title: Hierarchical Task Planning for LLM Agents in Web Environments
abstract: Web navigation agents based on large language models often struggle with multi-step tasks that require decomposition across abstraction levels. We present HiPlan, a hierarchical task planner that produces a tree of intermediate goals and selects actions conditioned on the current subgoal. Unlike flat agentic planners, HiPlan separates the long-horizon plan from the per-step action policy, allowing the planner LLM to reason at the semantic level while a smaller policy model handles execution. We evaluate on WebArena and a new shopping-task benchmark, showing improved task completion rates versus ReAct and Reflexion baselines, particularly on tasks requiring more than ten steps. We do not address memory; agents in our setup re-derive context from the page state at each step.
